phenomenon: geographical bias in locating social status
claim: Abstract spatial mappings are cross-culturally invariant at the vertical level, but higher-order spatial instantiations (cardinal directions) are flexible and determined by real-world distribution knowledge.

phenomenon: identity attribution shifts with performance outcomes
claim: In-group observers dynamically reassign which social identity a dual-identity target exemplifies, strategically aligning identity choice with outcome valence for group image management.
Systematic exposure to delayed reinforcement during development produces robust, lasting increases in self-controlled choice that surpass the magnitude of natural age-related maturation.

Physical effort (grip force) during conjugate stimulus access provides a brief, rapid measure of preference that corresponds to verbal preference rankings, offering an alternative to traditional assessment methods.

Organizational intergenerational climate is a multifaceted psychological climate reflecting employee perceptions of age-based attitudes, distinct from global job satisfaction, with implications for mentoring and prejudice.

Personality dimensions longitudinally predict body-weight changes through distinct mechanisms, with neuroticism and extraversion increasing weight more strongly in women, and conscientiousness protective at baseline only.
